title: ED mental health presentation documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.
red_flags.rationale: The exact RCEM audit standards support documenting onset or context, severity or functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, patient concerns, clinician risk context and focused mental-state assessment only when performed; they do not predict future risk.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental-health audit standards for the emergency department

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact triage and assessment section supports recording immediate safety concerns, safeguarding, clinician-decided observation or disposition, completed discussion and arranged liaison handover only when assessed.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental Health Triage and Initial Assessment

sources_used.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE law and pathways govern local use

source_limitations: Exact presenting symptoms, mental-state domains, physical causes, intoxication, capacity, current intent, access to means, protective factors, diagnosis, treatment, legal status and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
